title: PURIQ: A Master Formula for Agentic AI under Provable Provenance
description:
PURIQ (Quechua puriq, "the one who acts") is a single master formula that turns a governed language-model substrate into a provably-provenanced agent. PURIQ selects an action by maximizing the product of four factors: a positive-homogeneous, bounded Λ aggregator; the canonical 13-axis yuyay_v3 conjunctive gate (replay hash bacf5443…); an exponential penalty over ten HUKLLA halt-tripwires; and a Khipu Merkle-DAG receipt product that is nonzero only when the action's provenance chain verifies. The paper gives the formula's derivation, four governance invariants, and a Lean 4 formalization of the Khipu DAG soundness theorem, and tabulates 23 organ sub-formulas mechanized against Mathlib.

Honest scope: the Λ-uniqueness result is carried as Conjecture 1, not a theorem; the Doctrine v11 Lean corpus carries 749 declarations, 14 unique axioms, and 163 sorry obligations (112 baseline + 51 Putnam); this work is strictly additive, introducing four net-new sorry-tagged obligations (SORRY_PURIQ_OPEN[24..27]) and no new axiom; provenance posture is honestly SLSA L1.

This preprint is the canonical, standalone citation for the PURIQ formula, the agentic-action layer of Doctrine v12. It is independent of but cites the companion Ouroboros Thesis v20.
notes: Concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19944926 (Ouroboros line) resolves to the latest related version. PURIQ preprint source: szl-holdings/puriq-preprint, tag preprint-v1.0.0. DOI minted automatically via the GitHub-Zenodo integration on GitHub release; see ZENODO_AUTO_MINT_SETUP.md.
